name: Turkey Gravy
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Pan juices (add water if necessary to make 2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove liver from turkey giblets and set aside.
2. In a saucepan, cover the remaining giblets with lightly salted cold water. Add a few celery leaves and onion slices. Cover and simmer for 1 hour.
3. Add the liver back into the saucepan and simmer for an additional 20-30 minutes.
4. Remove the giblets and chop them. Discard the neck.
5. Reserve 2 cups of broth from the pan juices.
6. Skim off half a cup of turkey fat from the pan juices.
7. Gather the remaining pan juices, adding water if necessary to make 2 cups total.
8. In a saucepan, combine the turkey fat with 1/2 cup of flour. Begin heating.
9. Whisk in the broth and juices.
10. Whisk in the half and half.
11. Stir in the chopped giblets.
12. Cook until thickened and bubbly, then for an additional minute.
13. Stir in the brandy.
This makes a generous amount of gravy, plenty for leftovers. You can easily reduce the recipe by half if desired.
